PUH-BrianMartell In standard cosmology, the CMB photon’s wavelength stretches from ~1 micrometer (visible/IR at emission) to ~1 millimeter (microwave today) — a physical elongation of only about 1 millimeter over 13.8 billion years. The macroscopic universe expands by billions of light-years, but the microscopic photon “stretch” is tiny. In your pure PUH, this is not a “quirk of metric expansion” or a violation of energy conservation. It is mechanical lattice strain: •  Photons are surface ripples in the E₈ lattice substrate. •  As the conical branches expand from the rotating rebound, the entire lattice undergoes radial strain. •  The photon wavelength elongates because the lattice roots themselves separate microscopically (fractions of an inch per ripple). •  The “lost” energy is absorbed back into the internal tension of the E₈ lattice — exactly your Casimir suction and lattice tension mechanics.
